title: Two killed, 35 injured as sleeper bus rams truck on Agra-Lucknow Expressway
content: Two people were killed and 35 passengers injured after a sleeper bus rammed into a truck on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway

title: Karnataka government formation LIVE: Congress to convene crucial CLP meeting at 4 p.m. on May 30
content: “Nothing decided yet, we have called CLP meeting tomorrow”: DK Shivakumar on new leadership in Karnataka

title: Shivakumar’s elevation as Karnataka CM poses existential challenge for JD(S)
content: Party leaders weigh return of H.D. Kumaraswamy to State politics amid concerns over Congress consolidating Vokkaliga support ahead of 2028 Assembly elections

title: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah resigns, but won’t budge from State politics
content: Going down memory lane, he said he was an ‘accidental politician’, with none in the family being in politics

title: D.K. Shivakumar’s emotional note on Siddaramaiah: ‘A testament to resilience, perseverance, commitment to social justice’
content: Describing as a privilege to serve alongside Siddaramaiah as Deputy Chief Minister of Karnataka, Mr. Shivakumar said he had continuously learnt from the veteran leader's experience and political foresight

title: Haryana rights body seeks reports on woman delivering baby outside Faridabad hospital
content: The additional Chief Secretary, health and family welfare, has been directed to share a policy and mechanism for ensuring round-the-clock emergency maternal healthcare at government hospitals

title: Record 1.24 million devotees offer hair at Tirumala temple in 27 days
content: 12,43,063 devotees offered their hair between May 1 and 27, significantly surpassing the total figures of 10,18,370 recorded in May 2025 and 10,65,729 in May 2024

title: NEET paper leaks traumatic for students and families, institutions must learn, says SC
content: The NTA filed an affidavit in the apex court over the corrective steps taken following NEET-UG paper leak and said the decision to cancel the examination was taken in the interest of students and in recognition of the trust underpinning the national examination system

title: Cooling-off period not needed for good judges post retirement: Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal
content: The debate around cooling-off periods is often based on a perception that judges could be rewarded for favourable judgments, but such assumptions are misplaced, says Union Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal

title: Vande Mataram row in Kerala: Pinarayi says State ‘not obliged to enforce RSS agenda of replacing national anthem’
content: Kerala Opposition Leader says convention required that only first two stanzas of ‘Vande Mataram’ need be rendered at State functions, if at all

title: Kavitha opposes Rythu Discom, demands public hearing in 33 Telangana districts
content: TRS chief alleges the move is conspiracy against farmers and the proposal is equivalent to “poisoning agriculture”

title: Kerala Governor’s policy address lacks suggestions helpful to State, says Pinarayi Vijayan
content: Opposition Leader says he does not wish to be “harsh” at this stage, considering that new government is in its initial phase and that LDF chooses to observe the government’s actions for now

title: R.B. Udhayakumar warns Aadhav Arjuna of political consequences if TVK ‘poaches’ AIADMK cadre
content: Calling Mr. Arjuna a “recent political entrant” with no stable ideological grounding, the former Minister alleged that the TVK is aggressively trying to buy out loyal Opposition cadres using absolute money power and administrative lures

title: Financial institutions report over 10,000 cases of fraud involving ₹48,000 crore in FY26: RBI data
content: An assessment of bank group-wise fraud cases over the last three years indicates that although number of frauds for public and private sector banks has reduced, amount involved has increased over the years, say RBI Report

title: Kerala Governor’s policy address in Assembly envisions ‘Puthuyuga Keralam’ with emphasis on people-centric, clean governance
content: Primarily, the policy speech sought to expand on the poll promises of the Congress-led UDF, particularly the Indira Guarantees and the ‘Dream Projects’ aimed at transforming Kerala into a port-led hub and a civil aviation hub

title: Tiruchendur temple priest demands ‘bribe’ from HR&CE Minister during his surprise inspection
content: An order dated May 29 has also been issued to remove two security personnel posted at the entrance of Shanmuga Vilasa Mandapam, who allegedly received money from the priest to allow devotees for darshan

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Bhopal Court sends Samarth Singh, Giribala Singh to CBI custody for five days
content: The CBI plans to question the mother-son duo and verify their claims, especially about the events leading to Twisha’s death

title: ‘Vande Mataram’ row sparks political storm after Kerala Governor’s policy address
content: Controversy after Kerala Police band skips playing ‘Vande Mataram’ in its entirety at opening session of 16th Kerala Assembly. Governor Rajendra Arlekar takes strong exception to govt’s decision to play the song in part. BJP accuses UDF of caving in to pressure from Jamaal-e-Islami and CPI(M)

title: Amit Shah begins visit to areas along the international border in Gujarat
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah visits Gujarat’s international border to review security and engage with local officials and BSF officers

title: India to see 90% of long period average rainfall this monsoon: IMD
content: The monsoon which was forecast to arrive in Kerala on May 26 would be likely in the first week of June, the IMD said

title: Preparation, not fear will combat El Nino: Agriculture Minister Chouhan
content: El Nino is not something to be feared but something that we must prepare for, says Minister at inauguration of the Kharif Conference in Delhi

title: D.K. Shivakumar politicising Mekedatu issue, alleges Tamil Nadu Law Minister
content: Speaking to reporters at the Secretariat in Chennai, he said Karnataka could not conduct bhoomi pooja for the construction of a dam across Mekedatu since the Supreme Court had merely sought the submission of a project report

title: Kolkata Police to upgrade riot-control gear, forms panel to assess equipment quality
content: "Kolkata Police should be equipped with gear comparable to that used by Delhi Police, CRPF, and other central armed police forces," a senior official quoted the Chief Minister as saying during the meeting

title: Supreme Court allows Vinesh Phogat to participate in Asian Games 2026 selection trials
content: A Bench headed by Justice P.S. Narasimha said Ms. Phogat needs a chance as she has made the country proud multiple times in the past in the wrestling ring

title: Kerala moots three-day menstrual leave for schoolgirls
content: After university students, menstrual leave is proposed to be extended to school students under Project Menstrual Dignity, according to Governor’s policy address in Assembly. Students will get up to three days of menstrual leave a month

title: Karnataka Congress calls CLP meeting on May 30 amid leadership transition
content: The meeting will be chaired by Siddaramaiah in his capacity as the Leader of the Congress Legislature Party

title: ‘Kattalan’ movie review: A ‘Marco’ universe entry which is hard to sit through
content: Style overshadows susbtance in ‘Kattalan’ which belongs to the same universe of ‘Marco’. Despite a strong cast, Paul George’s film falters in all departments

title: Coimbatore child sexual assault case probe proceeding in right direction, no need for SIT: Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan do not find any reason to order a court-monitored SIT probe

title: Security forces fire tear gas shells to disperse protesters in Manipur's Ukhrul
content: Security forces use tear gas to disperse protesters blocking a highway in Manipur’s Ukhrul amid ongoing tensions over abductions

title: After Karnataka, Congress signals it has the appetite to settle Chief Ministerial feuds
content: Congress leaders Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi’s success in persuading Siddaramaiah to step down may now provide the party a template to handle similar leadership disputes

title: Confident that Manoj Jarange Patil will withdraw agitation: BJP Minister Radhakrishna Vikhe Patil
content: A delegation of BJP leaders — Mr Vikhe Patil and Prasad Lad, will meet Jarange Patil on Friday (May 29, 2026) afternoon to address his demands

content: Delhi Police said that Dhanendra Kumar’s son was also injured in the incident and is undergoing treatment

title: Migrant labourer beaten to death in Tiruvallur
content: The police have arrested three persons in connection with the crime and are on the lookout for others

title: CPI(M) activists protest in Vijayawada over ED raids on Pinarayi Vijayan
content: Raids were carried out as part of a planned conspiracy to intimidate the Left forces and democratic voices, alleges CPI(M) State secretary V. Srinivasa Rao

title: Illegal call centre busted in Goa, 26 held for duping U.S. citizens
content: According to police, the accused targeted Americans by posing as officials of U.S.-based banks through internet-based calling systems

content: The High Court restrained both the first informant and the applicants (Swami Avimukteshwaranand and the disciple) from making any statements before the media in this regard

content: Welcome to the Tamil Nadu Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow everyday.

title: Delhi HC refuses to immediately restore Cockroach Janta Party’s X account in India
content: A Bench of Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav issued notice to Centre and directed Mr. Dipke to approach the review committee set up under the IT Rule.

title: Taxpayers Association seeks scrapping of additional VAT, cess on Andhra Pradesh fuel
content: The association wrote to Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu and Finance Minister Payyavula Keshav, urging them to scrap the additional VAT and the road development cess on fuel

title: Tuberculosis death rates decline in 6 districts after roll-out of Kasanoi Erappila Thittam, finds study
content: The programme, which is India’s first State-wide and State-specific differentiated TB care initiative to reduce TB deaths, was launched in April 2022

title: Decision will reflect wishes of Viralimalai constituency voters, says Vijayabaskar
content: Vijayabaskar was one of the AIADMK rebel MLAs who voted in favour of the confidence motion moved by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay in the Assembly recently

title: SAAP, Tata Steel join hands to train A.P. footballers in Jamshedpur
content: Training will be provided to 80 boys and girls selected across four categories—Under 21-Boys, Under 21-Girls, Under 15-Boys and Under 15-Girls—with 20 footballers in each category

title: CPI(M) urges Tamil Nadu govt. to appeal against HC order banning cow slaughter
content: A summer vacation Bench comprising of Justices G. R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan had directed the Chief Secretary and the Director General of Police/Head of Police Force (DGP/HoPF) to ensure no cow or calf is slaughtered anywhere in Tamil Nadu either on the eve of Bakrid or on any other day.

title: 'PM Modi personally monitoring': What Centre, NTA told Supreme Court on NEET paper leak
content: Prime Minister Modi is personally overseeing the NEET UG re-examination process following paper leaks. The Centre informed the Supreme Court about new security mechanisms for the June 21 exam. The NTA has implemented extensive reforms, including enhanced CCTV surveillance and forensic analysis, to prevent future irregularities and ensure fairness for students.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reveals father's lesson on missed centuries
content: Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old sensation, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, is IPL 2026's breakout star, leading the Orange Cap race. Despite narrowly missing centuries twice, his focus remains on team victory, a lesson learned from his father. His selfless approach, prioritizing team success over personal milestones, has earned widespread admiration throughout the tournament.

title: A truce, $300bn investment & Hormuz: What's in draft deal that can end US-Iran war
content: Mediators are facilitating fresh US-Iran negotiations on a draft memorandum aimed at ending the ongoing conflict. The proposed agreement includes a multi-billion dollar reconstruction package for Iran's economy and a temporary non-aggression arrangement, with nuclear talks deferred to a later phase.

title: As US firm worry about AI returns, tech CEO says: AI right now works only for coding
content: US corporations are scrutinizing massive AI investments as costs surge and productivity gains remain elusive. Experts suggest businesses might be overestimating AI's current capabilities, particularly outside of coding. This realization is prompting a shift towards more efficient AI usage, with some firms even cutting jobs to manage escalating AI expenses.

title: Cake never delivered, refund denied: Delhi court orders Rs 5,000 payout
content: A Delhi consumer commission has come down hard on IndiaCakes after they failed to deliver a birthday cake, and subsequently made false claims about an attempted delivery. The online bakery has been mandated to refund Rs 1,031 and additionally shell out Rs 4,000 for compensation and expenses.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: Red alert for airlines? Middle East war pushes global passenger demand down 3.4% in April
content: The West Asia conflict has severely impacted global aviation, causing a 3.4% drop in passenger demand in April due to soaring fuel prices and airspace disruptions. Middle East carriers saw a drastic 46.6% decline. Airlines are reducing flights and increasing fares as a result of these ongoing challenges.

title: Smart Sunglasses Ban: BCCI bars devices in IPL 2026 PMOA; violations may draw penalties
content: The BCCI's Anti-Corruption Unit has banned smart sunglasses for players and officials in the IPL with immediate effect due to their advanced communication features. Players must now deposit these devices, along with phones and smartwatches, before entering the Players and Match Officials Area (PMOA) on match days. Violations will lead to disciplinary action and potential penalties.

title: NEET-UG paper leak: How NTA plans to prevent another breach — key reforms told to SC
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has informed the Supreme Court about significant overhauls to its examination and security framework following the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. New measures include mandatory CCTV checks, forensic analysis of footage, and contingency planning. The NTA is also considering a shift to Computer-Based Tests for future exams.

title: Amazon shuts down 'employee AI leadership board' & the reason is rising costs
content: Amazon has reportedly deactivated an internal AI usage leaderboard after employees "tokenmaxxing" to inflate their scores, leading to increased computing costs. The move highlights growing concerns for tech companies about rising AI expenses, as Amazon shifts focus to "normalised deployments" measuring useful code creation over raw token consumption.

title: GT vs RR: Sooryavanshi fear factor vs new-ball attack - what will decide RCB’s opponent?
content: The anticipation builds for IPL 2026's Qualifier 2, where Gujarat Titans face off against Rajasthan Royals, but one player has everyone buzzing – the 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. His explosive capabilities during powerplays have shaken up the competition this season. Can GT's ace bowlers, Rabada and Siraj, rein in this young dynamo and prevent him from orchestrating another unforgettable knockout spectacle?

title: Who is Shrey Parikh? The Indian-origin teen who won America’s biggest spelling bee
content: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old Indian-origin student from Rancho Cucamonga, California, won the Scripps National Spelling Bee after a dramatic spell-off against Ishaan Gupta of New Jersey. The contest featured 18 rounds over three days and ended in a rapid 90-second tiebreaker where Shrey spelled 32 words correctly. His victory continues a strong Indian-origin presence in the competition. The final, held in Washington DC, tested speed, accuracy, and composure under pressure.

title: Siddaramaiah out, DKS in: Why Congress' Karnataka challenge may not be over yet — what's next
content: Siddaramaiah and Shivakumar met Rahul Gandhi and Kharge before holding discussions with Sonia Gandhi at her 10 Janpath residence. According to party sources, the talks centred on the composition of the new cabinet, Rajya Sabha nominations and the role Siddaramaiah could play after his resignation. The veteran Congress leader is learnt to have conveyed his views on the new power arrangement in Karnataka.

title: Claude Lemieux death rumors explained: What really happened to NHL legend?
content: Former NHL player Claude Lemieux has died at the age of 60. TMZ Sports reported that he may have died by suicide, though no official confirmation has been released publicly. Lemieux won four Stanley Cups during his legendary NHL career and became known as one of hockey’s fiercest playoff competitors. Fans are also remembering his emotional final appearance in Montreal earlier this week.

title: Blake Lively hits breaking point amid Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce fallout rumors: Report
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have remained distant and private about their feelings for the Hollywood power couple, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, as they gear up to get married in the next few weeks in New York City. As speculations swirl about whether Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds will be attending Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s high profile wedding, it seems the actress has reached a breaking point in her life.

title: Claude Lemieux family post takes heartbreaking turn after emotional message resurfaces
content: Claude Lemieux’s death at age 60 has left the hockey world heartbroken. TMZ Sports and The Athletic reported that the NHL legend died by suicide. Fans also revisited an emotional old post from his son Brendan Lemieux after the news surfaced. The four-time Stanley Cup champion is being remembered for his legendary playoff career, fearless style, and unforgettable moments in NHL history.

title: Warships, spy flights & fighter bases: What the US military footprint around Cuba looks like
content: With rising tensions at the forefront, Washington has taken bold steps to strengthen its military arsenal around Cuba, sending in powerful warships such as the USS Nimitz and intensifying surveillance operations. The unfolding situation has led to a fuel blockade, plunging Cuba into an acute energy shortage that has serious repercussions for its citizens and economic stability.

title: Gavaskar's three-word message to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after touching feet goes viral
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 97 off 29 balls in the IPL 2026 Eliminator, after seeking blessings from Sunil Gavaskar, propelled Rajasthan Royals to victory. Gavaskar lauded the young talent, suggesting he's ready for T20 Internationals, while Aakash Chopra believes his India debut can wait.

title: Twisha Sharma death: How Giribala Singh’s background makes case extraordinary
content: A retired district judge, Giribala Singh, has been arrested by the CBI in connection with the dowry death of her daughter-in-law, Twisha Sharma. The Madhya Pradesh High Court revoked her anticipatory bail, citing allegations of harassment and pressure to terminate a pregnancy. The case involves a former judge and her lawyer son, drawing national attention.

title: US-Iran conflict: Can India shrug off impact? RBI confident of domestic resilience — key takeaways
content: Acknowledging the impact of the West Asia crisis, the RBI has said that several factors will help India counter the negative effects and emerge strong.

title: Kamikaze boats, like drones but on water; how small boats can sink massive ships
content: Iran's threats to close the Strait of Hormuz with kamikaze boats pose a major global shipping risk. Despite US efforts to neutralize Iran's navy, its small, fast, and hard-to-target mosquito boats, armed with rockets and missiles, remain a significant menace.

title: Jasprit Bumrah next Mumbai Indians captain for IPL 2027?
content: Following a dismal IPL 2026, Mumbai Indians face intense captaincy speculation. Fans overwhelmingly favor pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah to lead in 2027, with Rohit Sharma also a strong contender. Bumrah's successful interim captaincy stint and the team's continued title drought fuel the debate for a leadership revival.

title: How a mosque relocation may give Kolkata airport 10-year lifeline
content: Kolkata airport's capacity can be extended by a decade by relocating a mosque near the secondary runway. Experts believe this shift, coupled with runway upgrades, will boost flight handling from 33 to 43 flights per hour, accommodating millions more passengers annually and preventing economic stagnation due to limited airside infrastructure.

title: Kirti Kulhari's remark on domestic help’s Rs 10000 salary sparks backlash; Mini Mathur reacts
content: Actress Kirti Kulhari faced online criticism after a video surfaced where she questioned the salary of her domestic help. She expressed surprise at being charged Rs 10,000 for two hours of work, including sweeping, mopping, and dishes. Netizens and celebrities like Mini Mathur argued the amount was low, with some calling it below minimum wage. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Railways ordered to pay Rs 35,000 to passengers unable to occupy reserved seats
content: Four passengers with confirmed tickets were forced to stand on the LTT-Patna Express after their berths were occupied by strangers. Despite attempts to find officials and lodge complaints, they received no assistance. A consumer court has now ordered Indian Railways to pay Rs 20,000 compensation, ticket refund, and litigation costs for the deficiency in service.

title: HC issues notice to Centre, X on plea against blocking of Cockroach Janta Party account
content: The Delhi High Court has issued notices to the Union government and social media platform X regarding the suspension of the Cockroach Janta Party's account. The party's X handle gained viral attention following a campaign linked to a controversial remark by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, who later clarified his statement.

title: 'Part of RSS agenda': Pinarayi Vijayan says 'no need to sing Vande Mataram in its entirety'
content: Kerala's opposition leader Pinarayi Vijayan criticized the Centre and BJP, calling the full rendition of 'Vande Mataram' an RSS agenda. This follows a controversy where the BJP accused the Kerala Assembly of insulting the national song by not performing its complete version during the Governor's address.

title: SC invokes Article 142, issues directions to high courts to prevent delays in judgments
content: The Supreme Court has issued binding directions to all high courts to expedite judgment delivery and reduce judicial delays. Reserved judgments must now be pronounced within three months, with bail orders to be delivered on the same or next day. All judgments will be uploaded online within 24 hours of pronouncement.

title: CBSE Class 12th re-evaluation begins today: Check fees, steps to apply, important guidelines
content: The CBSE has launched its first fully digital re-evaluation system for Class 12 students, opening applications today, May 29, 2026. This new online process, supported by IITs and PSU banks, aims for greater transparency and accessibility. Students can now apply for verification or re-evaluation of their answer sheets after obtaining scanned copies, with fees varying by stage.

title: Ukraine targets Russian energy infrastructure; Moscow says over 200 drones intercepted overnight
content: Intense fighting in the Russia-Ukraine war continues as Ukraine launched drone attacks hitting fuel facilities in Yaroslavl Oblast. Russia claims to have intercepted hundreds of drones. Meanwhile, a Russian drone struck an apartment building in Romania, injuring two. The US remains open to mediation, while Ukraine's President Zelenskyy has appealed to Donald Trump for air defence systems.

title: Gujarat student stabbed to death in Canada; kin allege delay in bringing body home
content: An Indian student, Vidhi Megha, tragically killed in Canada, has her family desperately appealing to the Indian government for the swift repatriation of her mortal remains.  Her aunt and father expressed deep anguish over the prolonged delay and lack of cooperation from Canadian authorities, urging immediate action to bring her body home for final rites in Gujarat.

title: Amrita AEEE 2026 trial allotment out — check direct link, how to edit preferences
content: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham has released the AEEE 2026 BTech counselling trial allotment. Candidates can now edit their academic preferences for 24 hours until May 29. This trial allotment helps students understand their admission chances before the first official allotment on June 1.  Admission formalities will follow the first allotment.  The university offers BTech programs across multiple campuses.

title: Relief for seer Avimukteshwaranand: SC upholds pre-arrest bail in POCSO case
content: The Allahabad high court in March granted anticipatory bail to Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati and his disciple Mukundanand Brahmachari in a POCSO case alleging sexual exploitation of minor disciples. Justice Jitendra Kumar Sinha, while allowing their plea, directed both the accused and the complainant, Ashutosh Maharaj, not to speak to the media about the case. The court had earlier stayed Avimukteshwaranand's arrest on February 27 and reserved its verdict after directing the applicants to cooperate with the investigation.

title: Beyond Gayle, beyond Tendulkar: Sooryavanshi so extraordinary that Moody turns to Bradman
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is electrifying IPL 2026, shattering records and drawing comparisons to cricket legends. He's already surpassed Chris Gayle's sixes record and is being likened to Sachin Tendulkar for his composure. Former Australian coach Tom Moody even invoked Don Bradman, highlighting Sooryavanshi's unprecedented dominance in T20 cricket at such a young age.

title: Air India plane that stayed airborne for 9hrs was under repair for 6 years
content: An Air India flight to San Francisco returned to Delhi after nine hours due to a malfunctioning Traffic Collision Avoidance System. The Boeing 777, named 'Goa', had been grounded for six years at Nagpur's MRO facility due to a shortage of spare parts. Critical components, including engines and landing gear, were replaced during its extensive overhaul.

title: Down the rabbit hole: Bangladeshi buffalo 'Trump' pardoned – brief history of animal sacrifices
content: We live in the era of viral animals, from Larry the Cat, the permanent fixture at 10 Downing Street, to Moo Deng, the cute pygmy hippo in Thailand, and Punch, the lonely monkey whose inability to make friends made the world go aww. Donald Trump the buffalo might be the latest addition to this pantheon. But long before the algorithm turned animals into celebrities, humans were already turning them into gods, omens, sacrifices, scapegoats and pardoned prisoners.

title: Karnataka 'nataka' enters a new phase? Siddaramaiah meets top brass; cabinet berths on radar
content: A day after annoucing that he would step down as chief miniter to pave way for Shivakumar in accordance with the rotational formula agreed upon after the 2023 win, Siddaramaiah expressed his desire to stay in Benglauru than move to Rajya Sabha which was part of an offer pitched by the party high command. Siddaramaiah's move to "partly  agree" with the top brass, while holding his ground on not moving to the Rajdhaani for national politics largely suggest that the deveopment may mutate to become a complex quagmire.

title: Back in Tajpur, Sooryavanshi remains the kid who never forgives or forgets
content: While the cricket world marvelled at Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s breathtaking 29-ball 97 in the IPL Eliminator, people in his village of Tajpur watched it like just another day. From smashing bowlers twice his age as a teenager to rapidly making adjustments within weeks, the 15-year-old’s rise has stunned even seasoned cricketers but not the clan that saw him take his early steps in Bihar.

title: AI Giant Anthropic Nears $1 Trillion Valuation, Surpasses Rival OpenAI
content: Anthropic's near-trillion-dollar valuation puts it ahead of OpenAI, which was valued at $852 billion in March. Both companies could go public as early as this year.

title: Weather Today LIVE: India Likely To Witness 90% Of Long Period Average Rainfall This Monsoon
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Strong winds and light rain bring relief from heat in Delhi with thunderstorms overnight.

title: 'Indians, Pakistanis Take Our Job': British MP's 'Racist' Remarks Spark Row
content: Lowe's remarks soon sparked a controversy, with social media users calling him "racist" and reminding him about Britain's colonial past.

title: Delhi Teen Wanted 'Gangster-Like' Image, Flaunted Guns Online, Arrested
content: During investigation, police found that the accused operated an Instagram account under the name "CRIME" with the username "king_of_azadpur_001".

title: 18 Deaths In 48 Hours: Police Probe Toxic Liquor Network In Pune District
content: While 13 people died in Pimpri Chinchwad's Phugewadi and Dapodi, five died in Pune's Hadapsar and Kalepadal after allegedly consuming the liquor.

title: Twisha Sharma's Mother-In-Law, Husband Sent To 5-Day CBI Custody
content: Bhopal, May 29 (IANS) A district court in Bhopal on Friday sent former district judge Giribala Singh and her son Samarth Singh to five-day CBI custody in connection with the alleged dowry death of actor-model Twisha Sharma.

title: With 40 Lakh Homes Covered, This PM Scheme Steps Up Clean Energy Mission
content: Under the scheme, households installing a 3KW rooftop solar system can avail subsidies of up to Rs 78,000.

title: Karnataka's Next Chief Minister Can Give Vijay A Run For Money
content: India's political class is changing. The new-age power politician no longer feels compelled to perform austerity all the time.

title: Rs 20 Lakh Shares, Logs, Final Hours: CBI Probe Into Twisha Sharma's Death
content: Twisha owned shares valued at nearly Rs 20 lakh and both Samarth Singh and Giribala Singh wanted those shares transferred into their names, sources said.

title: 'FIR 75 Minutes Before Offence': J&K High Court Flags Lapse In Terror Case
content: The court said that it cannot distance itself from the striking discrepancies in the filing of the FIR and timing of the alleged commission of the offense.

title: Siddaramaiah's 2nd Delhi Visit In 4 Days. This Time With Son Yathindra
content: At the meeting with Rahul Gandhi, Siddaramaiah was not asked to reconsider his decision to join the Rajya Sabha, sources said.

title: Opinion: Despite Growing Revolt In Trinamool , No Sign of Introspection by Mamata Banerjee
content: After its Bengal poll setback, the Trinamool Congress faces growing internal revolt, public criticism, and resignations. Leaders allege centralisation, organisational decay, and disconnect from workers, even as critics question the lack of introspect

title: Japan Airlines Bans Crew From Drinking During Layovers After Flight Delay Incident
content: The latest case took place on May 23 when a chief flight attendant scheduled to operate a morning flight from Hiroshima to Tokyo failed mandatory breathalyzer checks before duty.

title: On Camera, Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin's New Glenn Rocket Explodes During Test
content: Florida congressman Mike Haridopolos, whose district includes Cape Canaveral, said in a statement on X that he has been in contact with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman regarding the explosion.

title: Twisha Sharma's Mother-In-Law Might Have Tampered Forensic Evidence: Court
content: Madhya Pradesh High Court quashes anticipatory bail of retired judge Giribala Singh in Twisha Sharma death case.

title: 6 Killed, 3 Trapped As Under-Construction Bridge Collapses In UP During Storm

title: NASA alerts massive 370-foot asteroid 2026 HW2 to pass Earth in high-speed flyby today at 44k km/h
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
